title: Upon A Lady Fair But Fruitless.
content:
Twice has Pudica been a bride, and led
    By holy Hymen to the nuptial bed.
    Two youths she's known thrice two, and twice three years;
    Yet not a lily from the bed appears:
    Nor will; for why, Pudica this may know,
    Trees never bear unless they first do blow.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "Upon A Lady Fair But Fruitless.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
